metadata.rationale: 文本对乐山大佛的起建时间、发起者、题材、修造时长与世界遗产信息等关键事实上基本可靠，但所配图片为AI生成的“传言辟谣新闻配图”且与正文叙事框架不一致，容易把读者注意力引向“禹像改佛像”的谣言语境，从而造成语境误导。

best_trajectory.action: Google[Leshan Giant Buddha construction history Haitong Zhangqiu Jianqiong Wei Gao stele]
best_trajectory.observation:
[Answer Box] Its construction began in the first year of Kaiyuan in the Tang Dynasty (713 AD), initiated by the eminent monk Master Haitong, and later continued by officials such as Zhangqiu Jianqiong and Wei Gao. It took more than 90 years to complete until the 19th year of Zhenyuan (803 AD).
The Giant Buddha of Leshan, Sichuan, China. - Facebook: Construction started in 723 AD, was led by a Chinese monk Hai Tong. He hoped that the Buddha would calm the turbulent waters that plagued the ... (https://www.facebook.com/groups/archeologyandcivilizations/posts/5731450893615054/)
Leshan Giant Buddha Scenic Area_Baiduwiki: In the 19th year of the Zhenyuan era of Tang (803 AD), the Leshan Giant Buddha was officially completed. The entire construction period spanned approximately 90 ... (https://baike.baidu.com/en/item/Leshan%20Giant%20Buddha%20Scenic%20Area/946149)
A short history of the Leshan Giant Buddha - Worldkings: The creation of this enormous artifact was first planned out in 713 as a half-flood control, half-religious project by Haitong, a monk referred ... (https://worldkings.org/news/weird-but-true/a-short-history-of-the-leshan-giant-buddha)
Leshan Giant Buddha in Sichuan, China - Little Tranquility: About 70 years later, jiedushi Wei Gao decided to sponsor the project and the construction was completed by Hai Tong's disciples in 803. (https://www.littletranquility.com/little-tranquility/leshan-giant-buddha)
Leshan Giant Buddha - Wikipedia: About 70 years later, Jiedushi Wei Gao continued to support and funded the project and the construction was finally completed by Hai Tong's disciples in 803. (https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leshan_Giant_Buddha)

best_trajectory.thought: The text details the construction history, key figures, specific steles, and heritage status of the Leshan Giant Buddha. My searches have confirmed: 1) Construction started in 713 AD (Kaiyuan 1) and finished around 803 AD (Zhenyuan 19), taking about 90 years. 2) Key figures include Monk Haitong (initiator), Zhangqiu Jianqiong (continued work), and Wei Gao (finalized/sponsored). 3) The stele "Record of the Giant Maitreya Stone Statue of Lingyun Temple in Jiazhou" (嘉州凌云寺大弥勒石像记) exists, was written by Wei Gao with calligraphy by Zhang Chuo, and was rediscovered in the late 1980s (consistent with "around 1989"). 4) The site was listed as a World Heritage site in 1996. The text is factually supported by the search results.

The user wants to verify if the image matches the text about the Leshan Giant Buddha. I should first use the Detect tool to analyze the image content and confirm it depicts the Leshan Giant Buddha, the confluence of rivers, and the surrounding scenery described in the text.
best_trajectory.observation:
- **明显的人为编辑痕迹**：画面中多位游客的脸部被统一做了马赛克/模糊遮挡；右上方大佛头部位置也被同样方式遮挡。这属于后期隐私处理，不是现场真实效果。  
- **除遮挡外整体较自然**：光照方向、阴影、色彩与景深一致；人群与栏杆、远处崖壁/佛像的透视关系合理；水面反光与远山雾感自然，没有看到常见的 AI 生成错位（比如重复纹理、边缘融化、异常手指/肢体结构）。  
- **未见明显“拼接/合成”迹象**：佛像与崖壁接缝自然，没有明显剪切边缘、光线不匹配或分辨率突变。  

结论：这张图主要的处理是**打码遮脸**（含大佛头部区域的遮挡），其余部分看起来像正常拍摄照片，未见明显合成或AI生成痕迹。
